pi_items.source: task.json description explicitly states 'Sort Order: 10' for Fit Accuracy; verifier.py checks for position == '10' (line 66); export_result.sh queries rating.position field

task.json description states 'Visible In: Default Store View'; Adobe Commerce documentation confirms Default Store View is created with ID 1 during installation; verifier.py checks for '1' in store_ids (line 60); export_result.sh queries rating_store.store_id via GROUP_CONCAT
task.json description explicitly states 'Sort Order: 20' for Material Quality; verifier.py checks for position == '20' (line 89); export_result.sh queries rating.position field

task.json description states 'Visible In: Default Store View'; Adobe Commerce documentation confirms Default Store View is created with ID 1 during installation; verifier.py checks for '1' in store_ids (line 84); export_result.sh queries rating_store.store_id via GROUP_CONCAT

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating two custom product rating criteria in Magento Open Source: (1) 'Fit Accuracy' with sort order 10, assigned to store ID 1 (Default Store View), active; (2) 'Material Quality' with sort order 20, assigned to store ID 1 (Default Store View), active. These are custom rating attributes that do not exist by default and must be created via Stores > Attributes > Rating in the Magento admin interface. Store ID 1 is the Default Store View automatically created during Magento installation. The task is verified against Magento's rating table structure (rating.position for sort order, rating_store.store_id for store assignment) and the Adobe Commerce documentation for rating attribute management.
